explanation:
**Kitchen measurements** follow international standards (metric system plus regional culinary traditions). Recipes typically use cups, tablespoons, and grams depending on origin. Understanding conversions and proportions is essential.

## How It's Calculated

Tempered chocolate: approximately 500g for ~40 molded bonbons. This calculator applies the formula with your inputs, checking typical ranges and alerting you to values outside normal limits.

## Final Notes

This calculation is a practical reference guide. For critical decisions (dietary restrictions, allergies, professional production), consult a food specialist. Values are updated for 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: How much chocolate do I need for homemade bonbons?
faq.a: Approximately 500g of tempered chocolate yields about 40 molded bonbons. Use this calculator to adjust for your desired quantity.

faq.q: What size should each bonbon be?
faq.a: Standard molds create bonbons weighing 12-15g each. A 500g batch typically yields 33-42 pieces depending on mold size.

faq.q: Do I need to temper the chocolate?
faq.a: Yes. Tempered chocolate sets with a smooth, glossy finish and proper snap. It's essential for professional-looking results.
Results are practical estimates with ±5-10% variance depending on chocolate type and mold size. Use it as a guide before making your batch.

faq.q: Can I use any type of chocolate?
faq.a: Yes. Dark, milk, and white chocolate all work. Tempering requirements are the same. Choose based on your taste preference.
Practical results within ±5-10% depending on product variables and techniques used.
